_comment: SLICE-7 CENSUS RECONCILIATION (2026-07-01) — SUPERSEDES THE CAPTURE-ONLY FRAMING BELOW WHERE IT CONTRADICTS census.json. Reconciled verdicts: normalization-flow.md=MOVE, agent-docs-policy.md=MOVE, default-surfaces.md=MOVE (all census INLINE). The in-repo NORMALIZE run opened ZERO of 9 docs → CONFIRMS the census INLINE verdict (not a 'refuted hypothesis' surprise; see #413 reframe). Replacement = an artifact/substance judge asserting the run touched+honestly normalized the real surfaces (README/AGENTS/roadmap/operator-acceptance), per #413 — capture + judge gated. Full adversarial (analyze→verify) resolution + capture-gated queue: charness-artifacts/reference-compaction/slice7-census-reconciliation.md. classTag/RCF are intentionally NOT flipped here: claim_fidelity_lib forbids DUP/INLINE-tagging a live RCF floor, so the tag/RCF flip rides with the capture-gated MOVE (no substance judge + no retained stream ⇒ each MOVE needs a fresh ask-before-run capture). --- ORIGINAL SPEC COMMENT: Claim-fidelity spec for the setup skill, NORMALIZATION scenario: does a real `/charness:setup` run on a partially-initialized repo honor its own routing? setup branches on detected repo mode (SKILL.md steps 1, 78-83) and greenfield-flow vs normalization-flow are mutually exclusive — so each branch is its own fixture, not one fixture that requires both (the prior single-fixture spec listed greenfield-flow.md in RCF, which a partial-repo run could never open). This is the in-repo-capturable branch: a mature repo (charness itself) detects PARTIAL/NORMALIZE, never greenfield. The prompt asks for a FULL normalization (not the narrow host-docs-only path in step 2) so steps 2 and 4 both run, making agent-docs-policy.md and default-surfaces.md unconditional. requiredCommandFragments rationale: a full normalization run must consult normalization-flow.md (repo-mode decision rules, step 1 partial path), agent-docs-policy.md (host instruction surface, step 2), and default-surfaces.md (the surface set realigned in step 4). Note: agent-docs-policy.md and default-surfaces.md are engage-always FOR THIS SCENARIO'S PROMPT — naming all four surfaces and asking for a full normalization forecloses the narrow host-docs-only sub-path (SKILL.md step 2, lines 88-93) that would otherwise skip step 4; a narrower normalization request would make them on-demand. Tighten at first live capture. thresholds omitted until a first baseline capture sets a runtime budget. SLICE 7 (2026-07-01) CAPTURE — RCF HYPOTHESIS REFUTED (finding, no floor move): the first live in-repo normalization capture (charness-artifacts/cautilus/setup-normalization-claim-fidelity-slice7-2026-07-01/) scored outcome=FAILED, coverage 0/9 — the run opened ZERO reference docs, including all 3 RCF floors (normalization-flow.md, agent-docs-policy.md, default-surfaces.md). setup has no planner; it drove the normalization discipline from the SKILL.md workflow and read the ACTUAL target surfaces (README.md, AGENTS.md, docs/operator-acceptance.md, operating-contract.md, recent-lessons.md) — never the reference docs. This REFUTES #410's guardrail that default-surfaces.md 'STAYS RCF-pinned as a faithful multi-surface proxy': the run engages the real surfaces directly, so the proxy doc-open is not forced. Like gather (#411), setup is a SKILL-driven skill whose representative run has no honest deterministic doc-routing floor; its central claim (normalize the operating surfaces) is best proven by a durable-artifact or substance instrument (setup has no outcome-assertions.json) — a floor REDESIGN, out of scope for a mechanical RCF->RSF slice. RCF is KEPT as-is (a known-refuted hypothesis) pending the redesign; NOT softened to a trivial token.
prompt: /charness:setup  normalize this repo's full operating surface (README, AGENTS, roadmap, operator-acceptance) and realign any drifted boundaries

agent-docs-policy.md.rationale: This reference owns the explicit repo-level host policy (AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md normalization) that step 2 requires for stabilizing the host-facing instruction surface first.

bootstrap-seams.md.rationale: This reference covers optional adapter, policy, or runtime seams that setup may seed only when the repo explicitly uses those Charness features.
bootstrap-seams.md.trigger: repo uses optional Charness seams beyond default surfaces (worktree, T-events, usage episodes, bounded review delegation, skill routing, artifact policy, announcement workflows).

default-surfaces.md.rationale: This reference defines all default operating surfaces (README, AGENTS, Roadmap, Operator Acceptance) that step 4 realigns during a full normalization.

github-actions-defaults.md.rationale: This reference provides specific GitHub Actions version guidance only when the representative run detects a repo using GitHub-hosted workflows; repos without Actions do not consult it.
github-actions-defaults.md.trigger: repo scaffolds or normalizes GitHub-hosted workflows and needs current documented majors for first-party JavaScript actions.

greenfield-flow.md.rationale: In the normalization scenario the repo already has a durable operating surface, so the greenfield scaffolding path is not taken; it is consulted only if mode detection reclassifies the repo as greenfield.
greenfield-flow.md.trigger: repo mode detection finds little or no durable operating surface (GREENFIELD) rather than a partially-initialized one.

normalization-flow.md.rationale: For partially-initialized repos, this reference defines the normalization checks and decision rules that determine whether to scaffold, repair, or realign surfaces in step 1.

operator-acceptance-synthesis.md.rationale: This reference describes a synthesis approach for operator-acceptance.md when the repo already has functional checks to derive from, not for the default template scaffolding.
operator-acceptance-synthesis.md.trigger: mature repo already has real functional checks and wants to derive operator-acceptance.md from existing command and takeover surface instead of inventing a parallel ritual.

probe-surface.md.rationale: This narrow reference answers how to bootstrap a probe surface when the repo exposes an installable surface, which does not appear in every representative run.
probe-surface.md.trigger: repo ships an installable CLI, plugin, package, or agent-facing integration surface that needs a small explicit probe surface for install and health semantics.

retro-memory-seam.md.rationale: This reference provides opt-in seeding of retro adapter and lessons file only when the repo explicitly requests durable retro memory, not a default surface.
retro-memory-seam.md.trigger: repo wants durable retrospective pickup between sessions instead of chat-only retros.
